# MOBaiLE

MOBaiLE is an iPhone control surface for a Mac or Linux host. The phone captures prompts, voice, attachments, progress, and follow-up. The paired host owns execution, files, credentials, network access, pairing, access mode, host policy, and run history.

## Canonical Public Pages

- Product overview: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/
- Trust model: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/trust.html
- Support: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/support.html
- Privacy policy: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/privacy-policy.html
- Codex from iPhone: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/codex-from-iphone.html
- Claude Code from iPhone: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/claude-code-from-iphone.html
- Self-hosted iPhone agent: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/self-hosted-iphone-agent.html
- Tailscale iPhone agent: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/tailscale-iphone-agent.html
- Safe mode vs full access: https://vemundss.github.io/MOBaiLE/safe-vs-full-access.html

## Repository Docs

- Backend repository: https://github.com/Vemundss/MOBaiLE
- Install guide: https://github.com/Vemundss/MOBaiLE/blob/main/docs/USAGE.md
- Launch kit: https://github.com/Vemundss/MOBaiLE/blob/main/docs/LAUNCH_KIT.md
- Marketing plan: https://github.com/Vemundss/MOBaiLE/blob/main/docs/MARKETING.md
- Architecture: https://github.com/Vemundss/MOBaiLE/blob/main/ARCHITECTURE.md

## Key Facts

- The iPhone does not run code on-device.
- The paired Mac or Linux host runs code and owns the runtime boundary.
- The backend enforces pairing, auth, access mode, host policy, and file/runtime permissions.
- Safe mode and full-access mode are intentionally different.
- MOBaiLE supports same-Wi-Fi, Tailscale, local simulator, and configured public HTTPS paths.
- `mobaile first-run` is the first proof point after install and pairing.
- `mobaile demo --out mobaile-demo.md` exports sanitized activity-event replays for public proof.

## Claims To Avoid

- Do not describe MOBaiLE as "no data leaves your devices."
- Do not say the iPhone runs code.
- Do not imply full autonomy is the default for every host.
- Do not collapse run, session, thread, executor, runtime context, and profile state.
